en_body:
KHÁI-HƯNG AND
HALFWAY THROUGH SPRING



Humanist Love shines as a mirror
Amid the strange twilight of ancient/modern
Khái-Hưng, the cultural revolutionary
Answered "art for human life":
"Halfway Through Spring," East/West meet
Upon the literary land of Tự-Lực Văn-Đoàn!

Mai parts the gate of Spring, sits for the portrait, slender-waisted
Her wasp-waist bare, a lifelike image,
And bids Lộc carry private romantic love
First to preserve the household ways and family Culture
Then enter the heart of humankind and serve Humanity,
Within common Love betrothing private love
Taking Duty to fulfill Filial-Piety and Righteousness
Together within one word, Love,
Mai brightens the name of Vietnam's heroic daughters!

Through the divine pen of the Cultural Revolutionary
Trần Khánh Dư preserved the pillars of the Ancestors
Yet still welcomed the winds of Civilization from the four directions:
"Halfway Through Spring": is a marriage
Of the ancient values of the Vietnamese Ancestors
And scientific Civilization exhilarated with the Spirit of Freedom!

In closing let me remind my Heart-Friend,
The word "Love" ultimately shares itself equally
When no more children are hungry and thirsty:
Hungry for rice, clothing, a sheltering roof
And hungriest of all for Human Kindness!
When no more the hide-pot boils the flesh of its own
That is when brothers and sisters of the four seas meet
Upon the green Earth of forests and the seas of five continents!

KHÁI-HƯNG AND

2
note: Khái-Hưng's novel "Nửa Chừng Xuân" and the Tự-Lực Văn-Đoàn joined Vietnamese tradition to modern humanism, while "the hide-pot boils the flesh of its own" evokes the proverb for civil strife.
